title: 10 best herbal teas for runny nose
intro_desc: Herbal teas for runny nose are infusions made from various dried herbs, flowers, and spices that can help alleviate symptoms of a runny nose. They can provide relief by reducing inflammation, soothing irritation, and acting as a natural decongestant. Some popular herbal teas for a runny nose include chamomile, ginger, peppermint, and eucalyptus. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages. Ginger tea, with its natural decongestant properties, can help clear blocked nasal passages. Peppermint and eucalyptus teas contain menthol, which can help thin mucus and soothe irritated nasal membranes. By incorporating these herbal teas into a daily routine, individuals can find relief from a runny nose, allowing them to breathe easier, sleep better, and overall, improve their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with runny nose because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and tighten membranes, thereby alleviating excess mucus production. Its active compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, have been shown to have a decongestant effect, making it an effective natural remedy for managing nasal congestion and related symptoms. Additionally, yarrow tea's soothing warmth can provide comfort and relief during cold and flu season.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Yarrow tea's astringent properties help to tighten and dry up mucous membranes, reducing excess mucus production and alleviating a runny nose. This is mainly due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids in yarrow.

Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. The active constituents responsible for this property include sesquiterpene lactones, especially alantolactone and isoalantolactone.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea has antimicrobial properties that help protect against infections that can cause or worsen a runny nose. The essential oils, such as azulene and α-pinene, contribute to yarrow's antimicrobial activity.

Decongestant: Yarrow tea can help to relieve congestion by constricting blood vessels in the nasal passages, promoting drainage and easing breathing. The flavonoids and volatile oils in yarrow are responsible for this decongestant effect.

Analgesic: Yarrow tea has analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with a runny nose, such as headaches and sinus pressure. The sesquiterpene lactones and essential oils in yarrow contribute to its analgesic effects.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the yarrow in a cup or teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain out the herbs, leaving only the liquid.
Sip the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Yarrow flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them suitable for soothing a runny nose.
Leaves: Yarrow leaves contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have decongestant and antimicrobial effects, helping to alleviate a runny nose.
Stems: Yarrow stems have similar medicinal properties to the leaves, providing additional sources of beneficial compounds for treating a runny nose.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are a good source of essential oils and may help reduce inflammation, providing relief for a runny nose.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with runny nose because it has anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties, which means it can soothe irritated mucous membranes and reduce inflammation in the nose and throat. The mucilage in marshmallow tea can also help to trap and remove excess mucus, providing relief from a runny nose. Additionally, marshmallow tea has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which may help to fight off infections that can cause a runny nose. Drinking marshmallow tea can provide a natural and effective way to find relief from a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tea's soothing effect reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose. This property is due to the presence of mucilage, a complex carbohydrate that forms a protective layer on mucous membranes.

Demulcent: The tea forms a soothing film on the mucous membranes, helping to alleviate irritation and reduce excess mucus production, which is often the cause of a runny nose. The demulcent effect is primarily attributed to the high mucilage content.

Expectorant: Marshmallow tea helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel the mucus and clear the nasal passages. This is due to the increased hydration provided by the tea, which thins mucus and allows it to be expelled more easily.

Antitussive: The tea's ability to suppress coughing can help alleviate a runny nose by reducing the urge to cough, which can further irritate the nasal passages. This property is also attributed to the high mucilage content.

Antimicrobial: Marshmallow tea has mild antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against secondary infections that can exacerbate a runny nose. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids, tannins, and other phenolic compounds.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried elderflowers, and 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat, and add 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves.
Cover the pot and let the mixture steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea, and drink it while it's still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and antioxidants, marshmallow flowers help soothe irritated mucous membranes, providing relief for a runny nose.
Leaves: High in mucilage content, marshmallow leaves create a protective film over the respiratory tract, easing congestion and reducing runny nose symptoms.
Roots: Packed with anti-inflammatory properties, marshmallow roots can alleviate inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to control a runny nose.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort tea helps with runny nose because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, mugwort contains compounds that can help break up mucus and clear out congestion, providing relief from a runny nose. Furthermore, mugwort has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which may help to fight off infections that can cause a runny nose. Drinking mugwort tea can also help to soothe a sore throat and alleviate other cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Mugwort tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for reduced mucus production. This is due to the presence of compounds such as chamazulene and camphor.
Antimicrobial: Mugwort tea's antimicrobial properties help fight off infections that can cause a runny nose. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as 1,8-cineole and alpha-pinene.
Decongestant: Mugwort tea's decongestant properties help clear blocked nasal passages, reducing a runny nose. This is due to the presence of compounds such as camphor and menthol.
Expectorant: Mugwort tea's expectorant properties help clear mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing a runny nose. This is due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and saponins.

Immune-boosting: Mugwort tea's immune-boosting properties help strengthen the body's defenses against illnesses that can cause a runny nose. This is due to the presence of antioxidants such as vitamin C and polyphenols.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down for 2-3 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried mugwort herb to a tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the hot water over the mugwort herb and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strainer and discard the used herb.
Drink the mugwort tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed for a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain essential oils and antioxidants that can help alleviate inflammation and reduce mucus production.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, they can provide relief from nasal congestion and soothe irritated nasal passages.
Rhyzomes: Packed with anti-inflammatory and expectorant compounds, they can help clear blocked nasal passages and reduce runny nose symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy tea helps with runny nose because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose. The herbal tea is also known for its antioxidant properties, which can help boost the immune system and fight off infections that may be causing the runny nose. Additionally, daisy tea has natural antimicrobial and antiviral properties that can help eliminate the viruses and bacteria responsible for causing nasal congestion and a runny nose. Drinking daisy tea can help soothe irritated nasal passages, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose, making it an effective natural remedy for cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Daisy tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and less mucus production. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of daisy tea inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, which can cause or worsen a runny nose. This is attributed to the presence of phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: Daisy tea's antioxidant properties help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate symptoms of a runny nose. This is due to the high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Immune-boosting: Daisy tea supports the immune system, helping the body fight off infections that can lead to a runny nose. This is attributed to the presence of vitamins C and E, as well as essential minerals.

Diaphoretic: Daisy tea promotes sweating, which can help the body expel toxins and reduce congestion in the nasal passages. This is due to the presence of volatile oils, such as chamazulene and bisabolol.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of fresh daisy flowers or 1 teaspoon of dried daisy flowers.
Rinse the fresh daisy flowers under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the daisy flowers in a tea infuser or teapot and pour 8 ounces of boiling water over them.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the herbal properties to be released.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea, then enjoy the herbal daisy tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, daisy flowers can help alleviate inflammation in the nasal passages, reducing runny noses.
Leaves: Packed with anti-inflammatory properties, daisy leaves can provide relief from a runny nose by reducing swelling in the nasal lining.
Buds: High in flavonoids, daisy buds can help soothe irritation and reduce mucus production, clearing up a runny nose.
Seeds: Containing essential oils, daisy seeds can help to clear nasal congestion and reduce inflammation, providing relief from a runny nose.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with runny nose because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing mucus to drain more effectively. The tea also has antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help fight off infections, which are often the cause of a runny nose. Additionally, pot marigold tea contains flavonoids, which are powerful antioxidants that can help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. Drinking pot marigold tea can provide relief from a runny nose and help promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose, due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties, attributed to essential oils and alkaloids, can help combat infections causing a runny nose by inhibiting the growth of bacteria or viruses.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea contains antioxidants, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, that help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can worsen a runny nose and other cold symptoms.

Immune-boosting: The tea can help strengthen the immune system, enabling the body to fight off infections that cause a runny nose, due to the presence of immune-boosting compounds like vitamin C and polysaccharides.
Diaphoretic: Pot marigold tea has diaphoretic properties, which promote sweating, helping the body eliminate toxins and reduce congestion, providing relief from a runny nose caused by colds or flu.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried marigold flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove marigold flowers, then let it cool down for a few minutes.
Add honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while still warm, up to three times a day, to help alleviate a runny nose.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with runny nose because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and soothe irritation. Chamomile contains a flavonoid called apigenin, which can help relieve congestion and reduce mucus production. Additionally, chamomile has been shown to have a mild sedative effect, which can help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, further aiding in the recovery process. Drinking warm German chamomile tea can also help to hydrate and soothe a sore throat, providing additional relief for cold and flu symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids, which help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating a runny nose.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of German chamomile tea, due to its essential oils and phenolic compounds, can help combat infections causing a runny nose.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, provided by flavonoids and terpenoids, helps protect the nasal lining from oxidative stress and supports overall immune function during a runny nose.
Analgesic: German chamomile tea contains analgesic compounds, such as bisabolol and matricin, that can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with a runny nose.

Sedative: The sedative properties of German chamomile tea, due to its volatile oils like chamazulene, can promote relaxation and help improve sleep quality, which is essential for recovery during illnesses causing a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the chamomile flowers and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the herbal tea up to three times a day for runny nose relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile flowers are used to make medicinal teas that help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, relieving runny noses.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and essential oils, chamomile leaves contribute to the soothing effects of medicinal teas, helping alleviate symptoms of runny noses.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile seeds are used in medicinal teas to help reduce nasal congestion and ease breathing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with runny nose because it contains high levels of echinacea, an active ingredient known for its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These properties help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off viruses that cause colds and other respiratory infections, further reducing the duration and severity of a runny nose. Drinking this tea can also help soothe a sore throat and alleviate other cold symptoms, providing overall relief and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-catarrhal: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps reduce excess mucus production, providing relief from a runny nose by acting on the respiratory tract and reducing inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, alkamides, and volatile oils.

Anti-inflammatory: This herbal tea has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate nasal congestion and inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby reducing runny nose. The active compounds responsible for this effect include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils.

Antimicrobial: Eastern purple coneflower tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to prevent and treat infections that may cause a runny nose. The active constituents responsible for this medicinal property include alkamides, essential oils, and polysaccharides.

Immune-boosting: This tea supports and strengthens the immune system, enabling the body to fight off infections and illnesses that can lead to a runny nose. Active constituents responsible for this property include polysaccharides, flavonoids, and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tea contains potent antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall health and well-being. This, in turn, may help reduce the severity and duration of a runny nose. The active constituents responsible for this property include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried eastern purple coneflower flowers.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or cover the teapot to prevent the tea from getting bitter.
Drink the tea up to three times a day for runny nose relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in compounds that help reduce inflammation, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of a runny nose.
Leaves: Contain soothing properties that help calm irritation and provide relief for nasal congestion.
Aerial parts: Not to be included, as it is a general term that can refer to any above-ground plant part.
Stems: May contain medicinal properties, but they are less potent compared to other parts, and their use is not as common for treating runny noses.
Seeds: Not typically used in teas for runny noses, as they do not have the same soothing or anti-inflammatory effects as other plant parts.
Roots: Can have medicinal benefits, but they are not the most commonly used part for treating runny noses.
Rhizomes: Not a part of the eastern purple coneflower plant.
Bark: Not a part of the eastern purple coneflower plant.
Buds: Not typically used in teas for runny noses, as they do not have the same soothing or anti-inflammatory effects as other plant parts.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps with runny nose because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and fight off infections. The tea is made from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which contain compounds called eucalyptus oil and tannins. Eucalyptus oil has been shown to have a decongestant effect, helping to clear blocked nasal passages and relieve breathing difficulties. Tannins, on the other hand, have astringent properties that can help tighten and dry up the mucous membranes, reducing the production of excess mucus. Drinking Tasmanian blue gum tea can provide relief from a runny nose and help promote healthy respiratory function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian blue gum tea helps reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate a runny nose by limiting the body's response to irritants and pathogens, due to its high content of eucalyptus oil and flavonoids.
Antimicrobial: The tea's antimicrobial properties help fight off infections causing a runny nose, as it contains compounds like eucalyptol and tannins that inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses.

Decongestant: Tasmanian blue gum tea acts as a natural decongestant by narrowing the blood vessels in the lining of the nose, thus reducing mucus production and alleviating a runny nose, thanks to its active constituents such as cineole and alpha-pinene.

Expectorant: The tea helps loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel mucus and clear a runny nose, due to its expectorant properties derived from compounds like eucalyptol and alpha-terpineol.

Immune-boosting: Tasmanian blue gum tea strengthens the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off the causes of a runny nose, as it contains antioxidants and other immune-boosting compounds such as rutin, quercetin, and various catechins.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5-10 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried Tasmanian blue gum leaves and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves and discard them.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, to help alleviate a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are commonly used to make medicinal teas for runny nose due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the Tasmanian blue gum plant are used in teas to help relieve a runny nose, as they contain flavonoids with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Bark: The bark of the Tasmanian blue gum plant is used in teas to treat runny nose, as it contains tannins that have astringent properties, helping to reduce inflammation and mucus production.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue felicia tea helps with runny nose because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate nasal congestion and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. The herbal tea is made from the flowers of the blue felicia plant, which is known for its natural healing properties. Drinking blue felicia tea can help soothe irritated membranes in the nose, providing relief from a runny nose and other cold symptoms. Additionally, the warm temperature of the tea can help thin out mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing the duration of a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Blue felicia tea contains active constituents like flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose.
Antimicrobial: The presence of compounds such as tannins in blue felicia tea aids in fighting off microbial infections that may cause a runny nose.
Antioxidant: Blue felicia tea is rich in antioxidants that help boost the immune system, enabling the body to better combat viral infections causing runny noses.
Decongestant: The tea's natural decongestant properties, due to essential oils like eucalyptol, help clear blocked nasal passages, reducing mucus production and alleviating a runny nose.

Immune-boosting: Blue felicia tea contains vitamins and minerals that support the immune system, aiding in preventing and shortening the duration of colds and other respiratory infections that lead to a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried herbal blue felicia flowers.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, to help alleviate a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils, the leaves are often used in teas for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate a runny nose.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids, the flowers contribute to reducing inflammation and easing nasal congestion in medicinal teas.
Seeds: High in antioxidants, the seeds can help boost the immune system and provide relief from a runny nose when used in medicinal teas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with runny nose because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing mucus to drain more efficiently. Additionally, licorice tea contains compounds that can help soothe a sore throat and alleviate coughing, which often accompany a runny nose. The natural expectorant properties of licorice tea can also help loosen and expel mucus, providing further relief from a runny nose. Furthermore, licorice tea has been shown to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the viruses and bacteria that cause respiratory infections and lead to a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate a runny nose by limiting the production of excess mucus caused by inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Expectorant: Licorice tea can help loosen and thin mucus, making it easier to expel from the body. This property is important for a runny nose as it helps clear out the blocked nasal passages. The key active constituent for this property is glycyrrhizin.

Antimicrobial: Licorice tea has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections causing a runny nose. This is important because it can help reduce the duration and severity of a runny nose caused by a viral or bacterial infection. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and glycyrrhizin.

Demulcent: Licorice tea can form a soothing film over mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation and inflammation. This property is important for a runny nose as it can help alleviate discomfort caused by constant nose-blowing and irritation. The active constituent responsible for this property is glycyrrhizin.

Immune system support: Licorice tea can help boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections causing a runny nose. This is important because a strong immune system can help reduce the frequency and severity of runny noses caused by infections. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried licorice root.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice pieces.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and soothe a runny nose.
Seeds: Licorice seeds are often used in teas for their expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus and alleviate a runny nose.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory ailments, including runny noses, due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat runny nose with herbal teas is by choosing teas with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, such as chamomile, ginger, or peppermint. These teas can help alleviate inflammation in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose. To maximize benefits, drink the tea hot, as the steam can help clear nasal congestion. Additionally, consider adding a squeeze of lemon or a dash of honey for added vitamin C and soothing effects. However, if symptoms persist for more than a week, it is recommended to consult a healthcare professional.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to runny nose that are treated with herbal teas include sinus congestion, sore throat, and cough. Herbal teas made from ingredients like chamomile, ginger, peppermint, and echinacea can help soothe irritated nasal passages and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, while ginger and peppermint have natural decongestant and expectorant properties that can help break up mucus and clear nasal congestion. Echinacea, on the other hand, can help boost the immune system and reduce the severity and duration of cold and flu symptoms.
